headlines.text: Trump says US may strike Iran 'even harder' but prefers a deal as ceasefire holds by a thread
All 50 states now average above $4/gallon for gas; national average hits $4.56
DOJ grants Trump, family, and businesses immunity from tax inquiries in 'lawfare' settlement
Senate votes 50-47 to advance resolution limiting Trump's war powers on Iran
Nvidia reports Q1 FY2027 earnings after bell today; Wall Street expects $79.2B revenue
Xi Jinping welcomes Putin in Beijing days after Trump's visit — China plays both sides
Trump ousts Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky primary via hand-picked challenger Ed Gallrein
Drake drops three albums simultaneously — ICEMAN becomes most-streamed album in a day on Spotify
WHO raises alarm over rare Ebola strain spreading in Congo; 134 suspected deaths, 500+ cases
Stocks rally Wednesday as oil tumbles 6% on Iran deal optimism ahead of Nvidia earnings

breaking.text: Iran's chief negotiator accuses US of 'overt and clandestine movements' signaling new attack as Trump says deal in 'final stages'

stories.hed: Trump Says Deal Is in 'Final Stages' — Iran Says He's Lying and Loading Missiles
stories.dek: Trump told reporters the Iran deal is nearly done while simultaneously threatening to strike 'even harder.' Iran's chief negotiator called US moves 'overt and clandestine,' which is diplomatese for 'we see you warming up the jets.' Meanwhile two Chinese tankers quietly slipped through the Strait of Hormuz with 4 million barrels of Iraqi crude, because commerce waits for no ceasefire.

stories.hed: Putin Flies to Beijing Right After Trump Left — Xi Is Running the World's Busiest Guest Room
stories.dek: Russian President Vladimir Putin arrived in Beijing Wednesday for a meeting with Xi Jinping meant to reaffirm ties, just days after Trump made his own visit to China. Xi is apparently the one world leader everyone needs a meeting with in 2026, and he is not complaining about it.

stories.hed: DOJ Settles 'Lawfare' Case by Permanently Barring IRS From Examining Trump's Taxes
stories.dek: As part of a deal that also created a $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' compensation fund, the Justice Department has agreed that the US is 'forever barred and precluded' from examining or prosecuting Trump and his family's tax issues. The provision was quietly inserted as a supplement to the main agreement, which is one way to do things.

stories.hed: Senate Advances War Powers Resolution on Iran 50-47 in Breakthrough for Democrats
stories.dek: The Senate voted to move forward with a resolution aimed at limiting Trump's authority to wage war in Iran — a first step that faces enormous hurdles, including a near-certain presidential veto. Democrats are treating a procedural vote as a moral victory, which is where we are in 2026.

stories.hed: CFTC Is Probing a Suspicious Surge in Oil Futures Moments Before Trump Postponed Iran Strikes
stories.dek: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission is scrutinizing a spike in oil futures trading that occurred just before Trump announced a pause on strikes against Tehran's energy infrastructure in March. Someone knew something — or got very, very lucky — and the CFTC would like a word.

markets.context: Stocks jumped and oil cratered Wednesday after Trump said Iran nuclear deal talks are in 'final stages,' while Nvidia earnings loom large after the bell.

gas.note: Gas is up 53% since the Iran war started — your wallet has filed for humanitarian relief.

popCulture.hed: Drake Drops Three Albums at Once — ICEMAN, Habibti, and Maid of Honour Hit Streaming on May 15
popCulture.dek: Drake released not one but three studio albums simultaneously on May 15, with ICEMAN becoming the most-streamed album in a single day in 2026 on Spotify. The rollout involved a 25-foot ice sculpture in Toronto that fans tried to break open with pickaxes and set on fire, which is either brilliant marketing or a sign that we have all gone completely feral.

genZ.definition: After Drake dropped ICEMAN on May 15, the internet immediately spawned a wave of AI-generated fake album covers and parody songs featuring Drake and Kendrick Lamar with titles following the '-man' suffix pattern, with Complex calling it a 'cinematic universe' of fake albums. Creators are flooding TikTok and X with tracklist parodies, fake rollout stunts, and AI vocal deepfakes of both rappers, making the meme ecosystem around the albums almost as entertaining as the actual music.
genZ.usage: Trump postponing Iran strikes then threatening to strike 'even harder' is giving very much Iceman cinematic universe energy — the rollout keeps changing but the album might never drop.

tldr: Trump is simultaneously offering Iran a peace deal and threatening to bomb them harder, oil prices crashed 6% on ceasefire optimism, and Wall Street is holding its breath for Nvidia earnings after the bell. Meanwhile Drake released three albums at once, gas costs $4.56 a gallon, and the DOJ made sure nobody will ever look at Trump's taxes again — a busy Wednesday by any measure.
